topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email sat on the screen, stark white against the dark background of the laptop. *Application Denied.* The words swam before my eyes, blurring into meaningless shapes. I pressed my palms against my temples, a dull, throbbing ache radiating behind my eyes. My breath hitched, catching in my throat like shattered glass. I started pacing the small room, a frantic, jerky motion that seemed to consume all the available space. Every floorboard creaked under my bare feet, amplifying the deafening silence in my ears. I snatched the paper off the desk, crumpling it into a tight, furious ball before hurling it against the wall. A choked sound escaped my lips—a ragged gasp that ended in a shaky, uncontrolled laugh. I couldn't breathe. The air felt too thick, too heavy, pressing down on my chest until it was impossible to draw a full breath.

[story 2]

"No. No, this isn't right." I repeated the words aloud, the sound thin and reedy, like a plucked string about to snap. My hands began to tremble uncontrollably, the fine motor skills failing me as I tried to steady them. I stood by the window, staring out at the indifferent cityscape, but the view was just a smear of color. A hot flush crept up my neck, turning my skin prickly. I began to rock back and forth, a small, desperate oscillation, my knuckles white where I gripped the windowsill. My vision tunneled; the edges of the room seemed to shimmer and distort. I started whispering, incoherent pleas to the empty air, my voice rising and falling in sharp, uneven bursts. I squeezed my eyes shut so tightly that white spots exploded behind my lids, fighting a losing battle against the overwhelming sense of sudden, complete collapse.

[story 3]

I kept rereading the rejection letter, my eyes skipping over the carefully chosen academic jargon until they landed again on the final, damning sentence. A sudden, sharp intake of air pulled my lungs taut, and I let out a sound that was half-sob, half-shriek. I dropped to my knees, the cool wood floor offering no comfort. I clawed at my own hair, pulling at the strands until my scalp throbbed. My entire body felt electrified, buzzing with a chaotic, uncontrolled energy. I began to bounce, a high, rapid rhythm that shook my frame. Tears welled up instantly, blurring the harsh lines of the document, and I scrubbed furiously at my face with the heel of my hand, smearing the moisture across my cheeks. The world narrowed down to the burning sensation in my chest and the relentless, pounding need to scream into the void.
